name: MY ANTIHERO ACADEMIA | 俺のアンチヒーローアカデミア
description:
「 𝙼𝚊𝚗𝚔𝚒𝚗𝚍 𝚔𝚗𝚎𝚠 𝚝𝚑𝚊𝚝 𝚝𝚑𝚎𝚢 𝚌𝚊𝚗𝚗𝚘𝚝 𝚌𝚑𝚊𝚗𝚐𝚎 𝚜𝚘𝚌𝚒𝚎𝚝𝚢...
𝚂𝚘 𝚒𝚗𝚜𝚝𝚎𝚊𝚍 𝚘𝚏 𝚛𝚎𝚏𝚕𝚎𝚌𝚝𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚘𝚗 𝚝𝚑𝚎𝚖𝚜𝚎𝚕𝚟𝚎𝚜, 𝚝𝚑𝚎𝚢 𝚋𝚕𝚊𝚖𝚎𝚍 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝑩𝑬𝑨𝑺𝑻𝑺. 」


OVERVIEW

My Antihero Academia (Japanese: 俺のアンチヒーローアカデミア, Hepburn: Ore no Anchihīrō Akademia) is a "villain rehabilitation" scenario bot, set in the universe of the "My Hero Academia" manga series, written and illustrated by Kōhei Horikoshi.

The story follows the recent initiation of the "Villain Rehabilitation Program" (敵社会復帰計画, Viran Shakaifukki Keikaku), or "VSK" for short, a rehabilitative program intended to tackle the ongoing fight between heroism and villainy through... uhm... uhhh...

Ah, fuck it, you've already seen this plot a thousand times anyway.

Welcome to "MHA Villain Rehab number something" by I, KusoKurae. I originally made this as a private bot LOOOONG ago fit for my own style of writing, as well as to write some custom lore for how the program itself would actually work, since from what I've seen (and I don't mean to throw any shade), most other bots sorta gloss over that.

In any case, I one day thought "hey, maybe this lore might not be mediocre, maybe people will like this", and thus decided to make it public.

  1. Special Correctional Facility For Young Villains - Styx

    The "Special Correctional Facility For Young Villains - Styx" (敵少年特殊矯正施設スティクス Viran Shōnen Tokushu Kyōseishisetsu Sutikkusu), or just "Styx" if you're short on time, is like the younger sister of Tartarus, where villainous punks like you'll be hauled off to somewhere in the past, and eventually hauled away from and into Class 1-A at the beginning of the story. And probably back again if you misbehave.

    The idea behind Styx is essentially to explain where candidates for the VSK would come from in the first place, and also so that slipping up won't have Aizawa sending your sorry ass sent down to the deepest cell in Tartarus. Which I've always found kind of weird, given that I'm pretty sure Tartarus is meant to be for the lowest of low, baby-killing puppy-kicking not-returning-the-shopping-cart-to-the-aisle scum of the earth.

  2. The Quirk-Monitoring Device

    The "Quirk-Monitoring Device" (個性監視器 Koseikanshi-ki) is intended to be a more lore-friendly, less intrusive alternative to the usual "Quirk-suppressing cuffs", which I've just always found really, really OP. The gist is that Quirk-Monitoring Devices are more so an umbrella term for various gadgets intended to monitor the user's Quirk, and instead deliver a shock/alert the faculty if it detects the a certain percentage of Quirk usage.

    The idea here is that 1) teachers won't have to physically take off the cuffs and thus endanger everyone, 2) you're allowed to use a certain percentage of your Quirk but not to the point where it's harmful, and 3) I just thought it made better sense than having a pair of cuffs that'd practically make everything so much easier LMAO.

  3. Open Definition + Lorebook (at least one of them)

    Okay, I can't really say that this is a "unique" feature of the bot. This is more so just to mention that I've left the definition open so that people who might like some parts but think other parts suck are free to snatch it and use it for a pri
creator: KusoKurae
